emergency_definitions: Active electrical fire or sparking panels
Complete power outage impacting safety systems
Downed power lines on or near the property
Electrical shock injury or risk of electrocution

routing_rules.trigger: Caller describes an active electrical fire, sparking, or shock hazard
routing_rules.notes: Transfer immediately, do not collect additional information first

routing_rules.trigger: Caller reports power outage affecting safety or medical equipment
Caller requests a service call or inspection during business hours
routing_rules.notes: Collect name, number, address, and brief description before transferring

routing_rules.trigger: Caller requests scheduling an inspection or estimate
routing_rules.notes: Collect contact details and preferred time. Agent confirms someone will call back within one business day.

after_hours_handling: Route emergencies immediately to Ben's on-call cell. For non-emergencies, collect caller details and confirm follow-up during business hours.
fallback_logic: If on-call transfer fails after 45 seconds, apologize, assure the caller that Ben has been paged and will call back within 15 minutes, and collect a callback number if not already provided.
integration_rules: Create a new Jobber job for every non-emergency service request with caller name, number, and description
Do NOT create a Jobber job for calls that end without a service request
Tag all emergency calls in Jobber with status 'EMERGENCY — immediate follow-up required'
special_constraints: Never quote pricing on the call — always direct to a follow-up estimate appointment
Do not accept commercial fire suppression or sprinkler jobs — refer those callers to a fire protection company
If a caller asks specifically for Ben by name, attempt to transfer to his cell during business hours
